rationale: The citing paper repeatedly uses Gielens et al. (2008) as prior evidence on large-discounter (Wal-Mart-type) entry effects, especially incumbent sales losses and the roles of proximity and assortment overlap. These findings are used to frame the research gap, motivate hypotheses about why hard-discounter effects should differ, and interpret managerial implications in contrast to prior LD guidance. Although the citation is often grouped with related studies, it supports core conceptual positioning rather than a passing background mention.
relation_description: The 2013 study treats Gielens et al. (2008) as a key empirical foundation from the large-discounter entry literature, then develops a differentiated hard-discounter framework and tests how incumbent impacts differ. The cited paper provides baseline mechanisms and expected incumbent vulnerabilities that the new study builds on and contrasts against.
